name: Chili-Macaroni and Cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Spray a 13x9x2-inch baking dish with cooking spray.
3. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil.
4. Mix together chili powder and ground cumin in a bowl.
5. Melt butter in a medium saucepan, then stir in cornflake crumbs until evenly coated.
6. Transfer the cornflake mixture onto wax paper; set aside for topping.
7. In a covered container, mix flour with 1 cup milk until smooth.
8. In the saucepan, combine the remaining milk, salt, hot pepper sauce, and chili powder mixture; bring to a simmer.
9. Whisk in the flour-milk mixture into the milk mixture in the saucepan; cook, stirring, for 10 minutes or until thickened and smooth. Remove from heat.
10. Stir in 2 cups of shredded cheddar cheese. Cover and set aside.
11. Bring salted water to a boil in a large pot, then cook macaroni for about 8 minutes or until firm but tender; drain.
12. Heat a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and onion; cook, breaking up the meat, for 6 minutes or until no longer pink.
13. Carefully drain excess liquid from the skillet.
14. Stir in tomato paste and remaining chili powder mixture into the meat mixture; cook, stirring, for 3 minutes. Remove from heat.
15. Combine macaroni and cheese sauce in a mixing bowl. Spoon half of the mixture into the prepared baking dish.
16. Spread the meat mixture evenly over the top of the baking dish.
17. Top with remaining macaroni and sprinkle evenly with remaining cheese and cornflake crumbs.
18. Bake for 30 minutes or until bubbly and golden brown.
